---
title: "Terranix Infrastructure Source Snippets"
---

# Terranix Infrastructure Source Snippets

This page documents the shared/local/cloud Terranix source snippets in a copy-pastable form.

## Overview

This document explains how the Nix modules map to the OpenTofu profile entrypoints, allowing the infrastructure story to be read without traversing the `nix/` tree.

## Terranix Source Layout

The Terranix infrastructure source is organized to support:
- Shared configurations used across local and cloud environments
- Local-specific overrides and customizations
- Cloud-specific configurations for AWS deployment

## Nix to OpenTofu Mapping

Terranix generates Terraform configurations from Nix definitions, which can then be used with OpenTofu for infrastructure provisioning.

The mapping works as follows:
1. Nix modules define infrastructure components
2. Terranix converts these to Terraform/OpenTofu compatible configurations
3. Generated configurations are placed in appropriate directories for OpenTofu consumption

## Usage

These snippets are designed to be copy-pasted into your infrastructure configurations as needed.

## Related Components

- [Infrastructure as Code](03_01-infrastructure_as_code.qmd): Core infrastructure provisioning concepts
- [Opentofu Infrastructure](opentofu_infra.qmd): OpenTofu-specific usage details
- [Nix Terranix Docker Artifacts](../nix/terranix.docker-artifacts.nix): Nix definitions for Docker artifact generation
- [Local Development](03_03-local_development.qmd): How to use the local development environment
